reasoning: A thorough search of news articles and Wikipedia up to April 2026 revealed no evidence of a Member of the European Parliament (MEP) resigning between June 9, 2025, and July 1, 2025. While the Huawei lobbying scandal resulted in investigations, police searches, and immunity waiver requests for several MEPs (such as Giusi Princi, Fulvio Martusciello, and Salvatore De Meo) around May to June 2025, none of them announced their resignation during the specified window. The European Parliament broadly resisted the efforts to lift immunity at that time. The only tangentially related resignation found was that of Jacek Tomczak, who was a Polish deputy minister who resigned in late 2024, not an MEP. Thus, it's highly certain that this resolves as No.
